word.definition: To emblazon something is to decorate its surface, usually with a name, slogan, or picture.

sports_fact.text: On June 14, 1998, Michael Jordan hit 'The Last Shot' over Bryon Russell to win his sixth title — 45 points in Game 6, the final shot of his Bulls career.

market.headline: SpaceX Is Coming Early to Your Index. How Worried Should You Be?
market.summary: Owen Lamont of Acadian Asset Management says the fast-tracking of mega IPOs shouldn’t keep passive investors up at night, but it isn’t great either.

quote.text: We’re going to have a UFC fight. We’re going to have a UFC fight — think of this — on the grounds of the White House. We have a lot of land there.
quote.author: Donald Trump

history.text: The French Army of First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte defeats the Austrians at the Battle of Marengo in Northern Italy and re-conquers Italy.
